content: The agent is a pure function — it never traverses the tree or decides what to work on. A deterministic script (the evaluator) runs before the agent, evaluates the tree mechanically (scan pending/, find oldest unprocessed pair, check Octowiki readability, check staging proposals), and outputs a structured next-action.json. The agent wakes up, reads the next action, gets a fully constructed prompt with Octowiki context and rolling window, classifies one pair, produces one output (proposal or discard), and exits. Dry-run means proposals go to staging/dry-run/ instead of being applied. The evaluator is the first thing to build — no LLM, just filesystem checks producing next-action.json.

provenance.raw_agent: It shouldn't traverse the tree itself — reasoning about what to do is where agents go wrong. The traversal should be deterministic. The agent gets handed a specific task and executes it as a pure function... A script runs before the agent and evaluates the tree mechanically... The agent never decides what to work on. The script decides. The agent executes.
provenance.raw_user: I think we should build some of the ground work for running the behaviour tree as a dry run. How do we envisage that working? If it were run every hour in a repo with an agent with a prompt, what would they do? Which parts can be made deterministic?
